recording_notes: Direct camera inside blue FLS trainer — first attempt on this third equipment configuration. 168s clip; high visual quality compared to V28-V29 monitor recordings but still slowed by repeated regrasping during placement and cutting.

frame_analyses.description: The first instrument enters the field and the needle-suture complex is brought into view, starting the task clock.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial setup is cautious; triangulation is established after a brief pause.

frame_analyses.description: The trainee is aligning the needle for the first pass through the Penrose near the marked target.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Several regrasping adjustments are used to improve the needle angle before committing to the bite.

frame_analyses.description: The suture has been passed through both sides of the slit and is being pulled across the drain to prepare for knot tying.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement appears close to both marks, and no tearing or avulsion is seen during the pass.

frame_analyses.description: The first knot throw is being formed with a double wrap and the knot is beginning to seat down onto the Penrose.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Likely left-hand led; two wraps are visible, but tail management is somewhat slow.

frame_analyses.description: The second throw is created after reorienting the limbs, and the knot stack is being laid flatter.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Lead hand appears switched to the right; the single throw looks orderly and seats without obvious loosening.

frame_analyses.description: The third throw is being formed to lock the knot stack before cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Lead appears to switch back to the left; the final throw tightens without visible slip.

frame_analyses.description: The knot is completed and the instruments are being repositioned to trim the suture tails.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: This phase is prolonged by multiple regrasping adjustments before the cuts are completed.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends appear cut and the instruments are withdrawing, leaving the knot seated on the Penrose.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot remains in place with no avulsion and no obvious gap at the stitch site.

knot_assessments.notes: Likely left-hand led double throw; two wraps are visible before the knot is seated.
Likely right-hand led single throw with visible hand alternation from throw 1.
Likely switched back to left-hand lead for the final single throw; the knot stack stays seated without obvious slippage.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Placement appears close to both target marks; deviation difficult to assess to sub-mm precision but no high-penalty miss.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gap, drain not avulsed, knot stack seated through the cutting phase.

technique_summary: The run was completed without avulsion, obvious knot failure, or visible loss of approximation at the stitch site. Needle placement appeared reasonably accurate and the knot stack remained intact, but the overall time was slowed by repeated regrasping during suture passage, limb organization, and tail cutting. This is a competent passing-quality run on the new blue-trainer station, with efficiency rather than correctness as the main loss center.
improvement_suggestions: Commit to the needle angle earlier during placement to reduce the prolonged regrasping before each bite.
Separate the active and passive limbs more clearly before each throw; several seconds were lost sorting crossed tails during knot tying.
After the third throw, transition more directly to trimming so the cutting phase is completed in fewer instrument adjustments.
strengths: Both bites appear to land close to the intended target marks with no evidence of Penrose avulsion.
All three throws were completed with an apparently stable knot stack that remained seated through the cutting phase.
Highest GPT-4o confidence on this trainee in the series (0.74) — direct camera removes most monitor-recording uncertainty.
comparison_notes: Tightest GPT-4o-vs-Claude score agreement in the series at delta 1.3 FLS (435.7 vs 437.0). Both teachers independently confirm V30 is a recovery run from the V28-V29 catastrophe. Claude's penalty (5.0) is 2.5 above GPT-4o's rubric-strict 2.5 — sixth video in the out-of-rubric quality/efficiency pattern (V20, V22, V26, V28, V29, V30). Phase split disagreement is INVERTED relative to V28: GPT-4o pushes suture_cut to 22.7s while Claude assigns 70s; the difference goes into placement and throws on the GPT-4o side. Same convergent-tails pattern (endpoint delta 3.8s) with internal allocation differing by ~50s on the cut/throws boundary. Both teachers see drain closed, no avulsion, sequence correct, hand alternation preserved. Confidence gap is small and in the expected direction: GPT-4o 0.74 vs Claude 0.62, both at the high end for the series due to the direct-camera recording.
